# Button

<!-- Generated by @create-component-md. Source of truth: Figma → .md migration. -->
<!-- Figma: https://www.figma.com/design/mhUvPg9tOjlvQvEW6glQhJ/Wi-Design-System?node-id=504-1780 -->
<!-- Generated at: 2026-07-21T16:16:00Z -->
<!-- Optional context provided: none -->

## Overview

Button is a pill-shaped, horizontally-laid-out action control with a leading icon slot, a text label, and a trailing icon slot. It ships in three visual hierarchies (`ghost`, `outline`, `primary`) and four sizes (`default` 48px, `medium` 40px, `small` 36px, `mini` 24px), each with its own padding, icon size, and typography scale. State is modeled as two independent booleans — `isDisabled` and `isError` — decomposed from a single mixed Figma variant axis that also carried two transient, platform-handled interaction states (focus-visible and pressed/active) which are not exposed in the API.

**Variant axes.** `hierarchy` (ghost, outline, primary) and `size` (default, medium, mini, small) are true Figma variant axes. A third axis ("Property 3": Active, Default, Disabled, Error, Focus) was decomposed into the `isDisabled` / `isError` booleans; Active and Focus are transient native interaction states and were dropped from the API surface.

**Composition.** One constitutive child role (leading + trailing icon, both instances of a component named `squircle`, node `55:8521`) is always present across every extracted variant; no configurable properties were found on it. See Follow-ups for an optional per-child spec run. The `Label` text layer is decorative in Figma terms (a raw TEXT node) but is promoted to the parent API as the required `label` string, since it is the button's primary content.

## Known gaps

- **[high] Icon-visibility axes are unverified.** Figma exposes two unnamed variant axes ("Property 4", "Property 5") with exactly one recorded option each (`"Yes"`) across all 48 extracted variants. Every variant renders both a leading and a trailing icon instance; no variant with either icon hidden exists in this extraction. `hasLeadingIcon` / `hasTrailingIcon` are documented as always-`true` — re-extract (or ask design) to confirm whether a hidden-icon state exists at all.
- **[medium] Ghost hierarchy does not visually distinguish `isDisabled` from `isError`.** Both states measure the exact same content color (`Content/Neutral`, `#a3a3a3`) on Ghost buttons. This is a measured fact, not a transcription error — flag for a design review if that's unintentional.
- **[medium] Ghost + size=small has an extra background token.** The `small` size tier of the Ghost hierarchy carries a stray `unofficial/ghost` white background fill; `default`/`medium`/`mini` Ghost variants have no background fill at all (fully transparent). Likely a one-off authoring slip in the Figma file — verify with design before treating `small` Ghost as intentionally filled.
- **[medium] The `squircle` sub-component name does not read as a general-purpose icon-library primitive.** It was classified `constitutive` by the designer during extraction (an explicit, authoritative choice — not overridden here), but the name suggests it may be a placeholder/mock shape rather than the production icon component. Confirm with design before treating it as a stable dependency.
- **[low] Voice/Screen-reader section is inference, not Figma-sourced fact.** `_base.json` carries no accessibility annotations (no aria layer names, no accessible-name overrides). The Voice section below follows standard button a11y conventions given the measured `isDisabled`/`isError` props; verify with an accessibility reviewer, especially whether `isError` should trigger any assistive-tech announcement.
- **[low] `size="default"` naming collides with the API's actual default value (`"mini"`).** Figma's variant option literally named "default" is the *largest* size tier (48px); the component's actual default variant in the file is `size=mini` (24px). Kept as extracted rather than renamed, to stay Figma-traceable — but call this out to any engineer implementing the prop.

## Follow-ups

- **Optional per-child spec.** The leading/trailing icon child (`squircle`, node `55:8521`) is a `COMPONENT_SET` classified constitutive — you may optionally run `create-component-md` on that node to produce its own canonical spec. Not required for this `.md` to be complete or shippable.
- **Re-extraction candidate.** If a hidden-icon (`Property 4`/`Property 5` = "No") variant exists anywhere in the design system file outside the extracted node scope, re-run the uSpec Extract plugin against a broader selection to capture it and resolve the Known-gaps item above.

**Cross-section invariants.** The four measured sizes (`default`, `medium`, `mini`, `small`) use identical corner radius (999, pill) and identical `layoutMode`/`HORIZONTAL` structure; only padding, spacing, icon size, and typography scale change. Color tokens are independent of size — the same hierarchy × state combination produces identical hex values at every size. `isDisabled` and `isError` are mutually reachable only through re-extraction (no variant models both at once), consistent with the API section's `generalNotes`.

## API

### Main table

| Property | Values | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `hierarchy` | ghost, outline, primary | false | `primary` | Visual priority / style. Source: Figma "Property 1" variant axis. |
| `size` | default, medium, mini, small | false | `mini` | Source: Figma "Property 2" variant axis. Figma's option literally named "default" is the *largest* size tier (48px) — not the API default value. The file's configured default variant uses `mini` (24px). See Known gaps. |
| `label` | string | **true** | – | Button text content. Source: TEXT layer "Label" (placeholder content "Label"). |
| `isDisabled` | true, false | false | `false` | Decomposed from Figma "Property 3" (state axis). Disables interaction and applies the neutral/disabled color treatment (see Color). |
| `isError` | true, false | false | `false` | Decomposed from Figma "Property 3" (state axis). Applies the error color treatment (see Color). |
| `hasLeadingIcon` | true | false | `true` | Source: Figma "Property 4" (single recorded option "Yes"). Every extracted variant renders a leading icon (instance of `squircle`, node `55:8521`). See Known gaps. |
| `hasTrailingIcon` | true | false | `true` | Source: Figma "Property 5" (single recorded option "Yes"). Every extracted variant renders a trailing icon (instance of `squircle`, node `55:8521`). See Known gaps. |

**General notes.** `isDisabled` and `isError` are decomposed from the same Figma "Property 3" state axis and are independent booleans — no extracted variant combines both, but the extraction found no explicit mutual-exclusion signal either (no wrapper frame, no naming containment). Treat as independently settable until confirmed with design.

### State axis mapping (Figma "Property 3" → runtime props)

| Figma value | `isDisabled` | `isError` | Runtime condition |
|---|---|---|---|
| Default | false | false | default (enabled, no error) |
| Disabled | true | false | `isDisabled=true` |
| Error | false | true | `isError=true` |
| Focus | false | false | transient focus-visible ring (native platform state); not exposed as a persistent API value |
| Active | false | false | transient pressed/active state (native platform state; only modeled on the `mini` size in this extraction); not exposed as a persistent API value |

### Sub-components

None. The only always-present child (`squircle`, leading + trailing icon) exposes zero configurable properties in the extracted evidence — see Overview/Composition and Follow-ups.

### Configuration examples

**Example 1 — Primary button (default)**

| Property | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `hierarchy` | primary | – |
| `size` | mini | – |
| `label` | `"Label"` | – |

**Example 2 — Outline button, error state**

| Property | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `hierarchy` | outline | – |
| `size` | default | – |
| `isError` | true | Applies State/error red content + Button/Secondary white background |
| `label` | `"Submit"` | – |

**Example 3 — Ghost button, disabled**

| Property | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `hierarchy` | ghost | – |
| `size` | small | – |
| `isDisabled` | true | – |
| `label` | `"Cancel"` | – |

**Example 4 — Primary button, medium size**

| Property | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `hierarchy` | primary | – |
| `size` | medium | – |
| `label` | `"Continue"` | – |

## Structure

### Anatomy

```
Button (root, HORIZONTAL auto-layout, pill)
├─ Left icon (INSTANCE of squircle) — leading icon slot
│  └─ Vector
├─ Label (TEXT)
└─ Right icon (INSTANCE of squircle) — trailing icon slot
   └─ Vector
```

### Root container

| Property | default | medium | mini | small | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| height | 48 | 40 | 24 | 36 | Fixed, not hug. |
| width sizing | hug | hug | hug | hug | Content-driven (label length + icons + padding) — not a fixed token. |
| padding (vertical, horizontal) | space-12 (12), space-32 (32) | space-8 (8), space-24 (24) | space-2 (2), space-8 (8) | space-2 (2), space-16 (16) | |
| itemSpacing | space-8 (8) | space-8 (8) | space-6 (6) | space-6 (6) | Gap between leading icon / label / trailing icon. |
| cornerRadius | 999 | 999 | 999 | 999 | Pill shape at every size — literal value, not a variable-mode "shape" collection. |
| layoutMode | HORIZONTAL | HORIZONTAL | HORIZONTAL | HORIZONTAL | |

### Leading icon / Trailing icon

| Property | default | medium | mini | small | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| icon size (w × h) | 16 × 16 (md) | 16 × 16 (md) | 12 × 12 (sm) | 16 × 16 (md) | Instance of `squircle` (node `55:8521`); identical for leading and trailing at every size. |

### Label

| Property | default | medium | mini | small |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| fontFamily | Noto Sans | Noto Sans | Noto Sans | Noto Sans |
| fontWeight | Medium | Medium | Medium | Medium |
| fontSize | 16 | 14 | 12 | 14 |
| lineHeight | 24 | 20 | 16 | 20 |

## Color

All tokens below are measured from `colorWalk[]` and are independent of `size` — the same hierarchy × state combination produces identical hex values whether the button is `default`, `medium`, `mini`, or `small`.

### Primary

| Element | default (enabled, no error) | isDisabled=true | isError=true | focus/active (transient, native) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Background fill | Button/Primary `#244ea2` | Background/Neutral `#e5e5e5` | State/error `#dc2626` | Blue/700 `#456cb9` |
| Label text | Core/white `#ffffff` | Content/Neutral2 `#404040` | Core/white `#ffffff` | Core/white `#ffffff` |
| Icon stroke | Core/white `#ffffff` | Content/Neutral2 `#404040` | Core/white `#ffffff` | Core/white `#ffffff` |

### Outline

| Element | default (enabled, no error) | isDisabled=true | isError=true | focus/active (transient, native) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Background fill | Button/Secondary `#ffffff` | Background/Neutral `#e5e5e5` | Button/Secondary `#ffffff` | Black/alpha-5 `#000000` (low opacity) |
| Label text | Content/Primary `#0f172a` | Content/Neutral2 `#404040` | State/error `#dc2626` | Content/Primary `#0f172a` |
| Icon stroke | Content/Primary `#0f172a` | Content/Neutral2 `#404040` | State/error `#dc2626` | Content/Primary `#0f172a` |

### Ghost

| Element | default (enabled, no error) | isDisabled=true | isError=true | focus/active (transient, native) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Background fill | none (transparent) | none (transparent) | none (transparent) | none (transparent) |
| Label text | Content/Extension/Navy `#1d4ed8` | Content/Neutral `#a3a3a3` | Content/Neutral `#a3a3a3` | Navy/500 `#3b82f6` |
| Icon stroke | Content/Extension/Navy `#1d4ed8` | Content/Neutral `#a3a3a3` | Content/Neutral `#a3a3a3` | Navy/500 `#3b82f6` |

> Ghost's `isDisabled` and `isError` content colors are identical (`Content/Neutral #a3a3a3`) and the `small` size tier measured an extra `unofficial/ghost` white background fill absent from the other three sizes — both are flagged in Known gaps for design review, not resolved here.

### Variable collection

The root fill is bound via the **"Colors semantic"** variable collection (`Light` / `Dark` modes) — e.g. `Button/Primary` resolves to `Blue/800` in both modes in this file. Light/Dark theming is handled automatically by these semantic tokens; it is not a component-level property.

---

## Voice / Screen reader

> `_base.json` carries no dedicated accessibility annotations for this component (no aria layer names, no accessible-name overrides). The states below follow standard button accessibility conventions given the measured `isDisabled` / `isError` props — treat as a starting point, not a Figma-sourced fact, and verify with an accessibility reviewer (see Known gaps).

### Focus order

| # | Layer | Description |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Button | The whole control is a single focus stop. |

### Per-state guidance

**default**

| Platform | Behavior |
|---|---|
| VoiceOver | Role: Button. Label: `<label text>`. Traits: Button. Hint: "Double-tap to activate." |
| TalkBack | Role: Button. Label: `<label text>`. State: Clickable. |
| ARIA | `role="button"`; accessible name from visible text or `aria-label`. No `aria-disabled`, no `aria-invalid`. |

**isDisabled=true**

| Platform | Behavior |
|---|---|
| VoiceOver | Label: `<label text>, dimmed`. Traits: Button, Not Enabled. No activation hint announced. |
| TalkBack | Label: `<label text>, disabled`. State: Disabled — removed from the touch-activation traversal. |
| ARIA | `aria-disabled="true"`; excluded from the tab order (`tabindex="-1"`) so it is not announced as a focus stop. |

**isError=true**

| Platform | Behavior |
|---|---|
| VoiceOver | Label: `<label text>, error`. Hint: "Double-tap to activate." Error styling is color-only — pair with a text/live-region announcement elsewhere in the flow if the error must be announced. |
| TalkBack | State: Clickable — no distinct TalkBack state for `isError`; the red styling alone is not exposed to assistive tech. |
| ARIA | No `aria-invalid` on the button itself (that attribute belongs to form fields). If this button submits an invalid form, pair with the field's own `aria-invalid`/`aria-describedby` rather than trying to announce error state from the button. |

**Precedence note.** `isDisabled` suppresses the button's activation hint/state entirely; `isError` does not change interactivity, only color and (optionally) an externally-triggered announcement. No extracted variant sets both simultaneously.

---

## Cross-references

- **API ↔ Structure:** `size` (API) drives every row in the Root container / Leading icon / Trailing icon / Label tables (Structure).
- **API ↔ Color:** `hierarchy` (API) selects the Color section (Primary / Outline / Ghost); `isDisabled` / `isError` select the column within that section.
- **API ↔ Voice:** `isDisabled` and `isError` each have a matching Voice per-state entry; the API's `generalNotes` precedence statement is echoed in the Voice "Precedence note."
